matched_markets: Will annual inflation increase by 3.8% in April?

signals.headline: YES** — This represents a significant acceleration from February's 2
signals.raw: YES** — This represents a significant acceleration from February's 2.4% reading and exceeded expectations, primarily due to the US-Israel war driving gas prices above $4 per gallon[1].

signals.headline: **Energy surge:** Rose 10
signals.raw: **Energy surge:** Rose 10.9% monthly, with gasoline up 21.2%, accounting for ~75% of the monthly increase[1]

signals.headline: Market implications:**
signals.raw:
Market implications:**
This data shifted expectations about Federal Reserve policy. According to S&P Global's April outlook, rate cuts previously expected from the Fed in 2026 have now been **pushed back to 2027**, while the Bank of England's anticipated cuts were similarly delayed[3]. The energy crisis and broadening price pressures have fundamentally altered monetary policy forecasts globally.
